summary: First end-to-end test iteration for Accutek Solar. Backend: 23/23 pytest cases passed covering public endpoints (company-info, service-area incl. 17 counties 10IN+7IL, county slug 200/404, testimonials=4, faq=7), lead qualify (hot/nurture tiers + estimate fields), lead submit (TCPA enforced 400, success returns lead_id+score+tier), auth (login success+me, wrong pw 401, refresh, logout, brute-force lockout 5x401 then 429), and admin (list/filter/patch/sync-hcp mocked/stats). Frontend: home page renders hero, counters (1,247+, $24M+, 98%, 32), all 6 services, FAQ, service area teaser; quote wizard 3 steps work with validation, sliders/pills, 25-yr savings ($47,277), TCPA gating, success toast and thank-you state; /tools/calculator slider + residential/commercial toggle work; /service-area lists 10 IN + 7 IL counties; /service-area/vermillion-county-in detail page loads; admin/login -> /admin dashboard shows stats cards + leads table; drawer opens on row click with Mark Contacted (status updated to CONTACTED), Sync to HCP (mock-synced toast + HCP id mock_xxx displayed), Mark Won, Mark Lost buttons; logout works. Mocked items (HCP sync + hot-lead email) behave per spec.

integration_issues.issue: After clicking Logout, page stays on /admin (URL unchanged) instead of redirecting to /admin/login. Cookies cleared on backend, but if the user reloads they will be re-redirected. UX could be improved with explicit router.push('/admin/login').

issues: Public marketing site header (Home/Services/Service Area/Calculator/About + Get Free Quote CTA) is rendered above the admin login card. Per spec, header/sticky CTA should be hidden on /admin routes. /admin (dashboard) correctly omits the public header, but /admin/login still includes it.

action_items: Optional: redirect to /admin/login after logout click in admin dashboard for better UX (currently the URL stays at /admin).
Optional: hide the public marketing Site Header on /admin/* routes (including /admin/login) — currently /admin/login renders both the public header and the admin login form.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 647 lines — borderline acceptable but could be split into routers (auth, leads, public, admin) for maintainability.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in newer FastAPI; consider migrating to lifespan handlers.
Brute-force lockout uses MongoDB updates — acceptable but Redis would be faster at scale.
ObjectId is correctly excluded from all responses via {'_id': 0} projections — good.
verify_password catches all exceptions broadly; consider narrower exception handling.
stat testid 'stat-won-%' contains an unusual '%' character — works but consider 'stat-won-pct' for cleanliness.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (one minor UX nit on admin logout + admin/login layout)

seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_ prefixed leads via /api/leads/submit during testing (visible in admin dashboard). Safe to clean up if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid and stable; all 23 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py pass. Re-run via: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Note that the brute_force test uses a unique email each run so it won't lock out the admin. The only follow-up items are two minor frontend UX issues (logout redirect, hide public header on /admin/login) — neither is blocking. Mocked features (Housecall Pro sync, hot-lead email) are confirmed working as mocked per spec.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing tests in this iteration.
